name: Sportradar Group AG

profile.description: Sportradar provides sports data, statistics, odds, and streaming services to sportsbooks, media companies, and sports leagues. The company collects real-time data from more than 850,000 events annually across 90+ sports and supplies it to 900+ betting operators and 400+ media companies worldwide, with official partnerships with the NBA, NHL, MLB, FIFA, and ATP/WTA.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Sportradar has transitioned from a pure data provider to a full sports technology platform, with FY2025 revenue of EUR 1.29B (+17% YoY) and 2026 guidance of 23-25% constant-currency growth, driven by U.S. expansion and the IMG ARENA acquisition closed November 2025 adding 38,000 official data events [1][2]. AI Deployment (2/2): Sportradar appointed Behshad Behzadi as Chief Technology & AI Officer in 2025 to drive AI-powered personalization, with AI tools deployed in integrity monitoring, odds generation, and content creation [3]. Market Share (2/2): Sportradar is the category leader in sports data distribution with official rights to NBA, NHL, MLB, FIFA, and ATP/WTA; FIFA renewal and IMG ARENA deal solidified 70+ rights partnerships [1][2]. Pipeline (1/2): Plans to exceed 700,000 streamed events in 2026 and expansion into prediction markets; 2026 revenue guidance of $1.56-$1.58B provides visibility [1][2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is in line with sector average but not standout vs. data-platform peers.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Sportradar's mission to 'transform the sports ecosystem through trusted data and technology' is clear but not transformative [3]. Community Impact (1/2): Integrity services and partnerships with anti-match-fixing bodies qualify as general sector philanthropy; 100% equity/diversity training completion cited in ESG report, but no standout charitable scale [4]. Employee Retention (0/2): Glassdoor reviews describe leadership as 'deeply entrenched, risk-averse, and out of sync with the pace of innovation', with reports of 'dictated' top-down management and uneven workload distribution [3]. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder-CEO Carsten Koerl retains substantial equity as controlling shareholder, though no meaningful recent net insider buying reported in the last 12 months.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Sportradar's balance sheet is exceptionally clean with D/E of ~6% and 17% YoY revenue growth on FY2025 revenue of EUR 1.29B, accelerating to 23-25% guided in 2026 [1][2][5]. ROE of 9.95% is below the 15% target but reflects ongoing reinvestment; profit margin of 7.72% is still maturing as operating leverage builds (adj. EBITDA margin expanded 291 bps to 23.0% in 2025) [1][5]. Forward P/E of 36.6x prices in above-industry growth but compresses the metric score. Liquidity floor easily cleared at ~1.5M avg daily volume x $15.24 stock price = ~$23M [5].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Sportradar's core business of real-time sports data collection requires physical scouts at 850,000+ events plus exclusive league rights (NBA, NHL, MLB, FIFA) that AI cannot replicate — these data rights are a regulated, exclusive moat [1][3]. AI actually enhances the business via integrity monitoring, personalized odds, and content generation; however, some analyst lines (auto-generated content, media services) face commoditization from generative AI [3]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Sportradar is actively shipping AI products — AI-powered personalization under new CTO Behzadi, AI integrity monitoring, and generative content solutions are monetized across 900+ betting operators [3].

infinite_game.summary: Founder-CEO Carsten Koerl has built Sportradar over 20+ years with a clear technology-first vision, but Glassdoor reviews of a top-down, dictatorial culture and concerns about deeply entrenched leadership temper the infinite game assessment. The company plays to keep playing in sports data — long-term league partnerships and multi-decade integrity infrastructure — but the cultural signals point to finite tendencies.

just_cause.analysis: Sportradar's cause of bringing trust, transparency, and technology to the sports ecosystem is distinctive and partially lived through integrity monitoring partnerships with UEFA, FIFA, and the IOC [3][4]. The cause has endured through Koerl's 20+ year tenure but has not yet been tested through major leadership transition.

trusting_teams.analysis: Glassdoor reviews explicitly describe a 'toxic' culture with 'little regard for employee well-being', leadership that runs the company via 'point-to-point Zoom calls', and unequal workload distribution creating burnout [3]. These signals point to a low-trust environment rather than Sinek's Circle of Safety.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Management commentary in earnings calls routinely references Genius Sports as a worthy rival and uses competitive dynamics (FIFA rights win, IMG ARENA acquisition) to strengthen their own platform [1][2]. The product roadmap — AI personalization, prediction markets — is explicitly framed as learning from betting-operator needs.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Sportradar has evolved from pure data aggregation to streaming, AV content, managed trading services, and now prediction markets — the IMG ARENA acquisition added 38,000 data events and represents the biggest strategic bet in company history [2]. Still incremental rather than transformative.

courage_to_lead.analysis: The FIFA rights renewal and IMG ARENA acquisition show willingness to deploy capital aggressively during macro uncertainty [2]. However, the stock's 52% decline from 52-week highs and Glassdoor commentary on risk-averse leadership suggest courage is inconsistent [3][5].

investment_thesis: Sportradar is the global category leader in sports data distribution with an unmatched portfolio of exclusive rights (NBA, NHL, MLB, FIFA, ATP/WTA) that form a genuine competitive moat — rights AI cannot replicate and that regenerate revenue with every new betting jurisdiction that legalizes. FY2025 revenue of EUR 1.29B grew 17% YoY and adjusted EBITDA grew 33% to EUR 297M with 291 bps of margin expansion, and management guided 2026 for 23-25% constant-currency growth ($1.56-$1.58B) plus adjusted EBITDA of $390-$400M (+34-37%) [1][2]. The IMG ARENA acquisition in November 2025 added 38,000 official data events and 70 rights holders, and U.S. revenue grew 23% to ~25% of sales as more states legalize sports betting. The balance sheet is pristine with D/E of ~6%, giving management flexibility to pursue M&A and buybacks while AI investments under new CTO Behshad Behzadi unlock personalization and generative content monetization [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on culture, valuation, and execution. Glassdoor reviews consistently describe a top-down, dictatorial leadership style under founder Carsten Koerl with reports of a 'toxic' culture, risk-averse senior leadership, and unequal workload distribution that drives burnout — cultural signals that typically precede talent attrition [3]. The stock has declined 52% from its 52-week high of $32.22 to $15.24, reflecting investor concern about the IMG ARENA integration risk, FX headwinds, and slower-than-hoped margin expansion [5]. At 36.6x forward P/E, SRAD is priced for flawless execution on 23-25% revenue growth, leaving no cushion if U.S. betting operators consolidate pricing power or if Genius Sports gains share. ROE of 9.95% and profit margin of 7.72% remain below best-in-class SaaS benchmarks, and the heavy reliance on gambling-industry customers creates regulatory tail risk in jurisdictions where sports betting faces political backlash.
key_catalysts: 2026 revenue guidance of $1.56-$1.58B (23-25% constant-currency growth) and adjusted EBITDA of $390-$400M (+34-37%) with 200-225 bps of margin expansion [1][2]
IMG ARENA acquisition (closed November 2025) integration adding 38,000 official data events and 70 rights holders across sports betting [2]
AI-powered personalization and generative content products under new CTO Behshad Behzadi driving ARPU expansion across 900+ betting operators [3]
key_risks: Toxic culture and founder-CEO concentration risk — Glassdoor reviews describe 'dictated' top-down management and risk-averse leadership that could drive key talent attrition [3]
Valuation risk at 36.6x forward P/E with profit margin of 7.72% and ROE of 9.95% — any miss on the 23-25% guidance would compress the multiple meaningfully [5]
Regulatory backlash against sports betting in the U.S. or Europe could constrain customer TAM; gambling classification also limits Sportradar's positive ESG attribution [4]
sources: Investing.com — Sportradar reports 17% revenue growth to EUR 1.29B in 2025, March 2026

Sportradar Investor Relations / Glassdoor reviews — CTO Behzadi appointment, leadership and culture commentary, 2025-2026
Sportradar ESG / Sustainability Report FY2025 — cloud migration, equity training, diversity ERGs
